title: Robots Exclusion Protocol, Failure Semantics, Caching, and Sitemaps
description: The crawler fetches robots.txt from the wrong path, merges groups incorrectly, treats an outage as permission, obeys nonstandard delay blindly, and trusts cross-host sitemap URLs. Learners cumulatively produce RFC 9309 robots and sitemap decision record through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Fetch `/robots.txt` at the correct service origin and use an honest stable product token and contact identity.
Apply RFC 9309 group selection, rule matching, percent encoding, longest-match precedence, and allow-over-disallow tie behavior.
Handle successful, unavailable, unreachable, redirect, oversized, and stale robots responses with explicit cache and conservative stop policy.
